headline: A $1.6 Trillion Disruption: Why Wall Street Is Worried About a SpaceX Phone
summary: Verizon stock falls while AT&T and T-Mobile also decline as the investors continue to worry about Elon Musk and SpaceX encroaching into the sector.

headline: 3 Cash-Producing Stocks with Open Questions
summary: A company that generates cash isn’t automatically a winner. Some businesses stockpile cash but fail to reinvest wisely, limiting their ability to expand.

headline: Big Tech Catches a Bid But Microsoft Is Walking Toward Its Worst Month Since 2008
summary: Wall Street finally exhaled on Monday with a broad tech rebound, but the largest software name in the world refused to come along. Alphabet stepped into the Dow, SpaceX into the Russell, and Rocket Lab paid eight billion dollars to take on a satellite incumbent, a session full of headlines, but only one of them was strange.

headline: Is Charter (CHTR) Trading Index Status for Deeper Streaming Integration to Reinforce Its Moat?
summary: Charter Communications was recently removed from the NASDAQ-100 Index, while earlier it announced that Spectrum customers can now purchase Netflix directly through The Spectrum App Store, expanding its streaming marketplace integration. This combination of index removal and deeper Netflix integration highlights how Charter’s capital markets profile and streaming aggregation strategy are evolving at the same time. We’ll now explore how Charter’s removal from the NASDAQ-100 and its expanded...
